Transcrição Completa: 5 Sales Questions That Unlock Hidden Pain (And Close More Deals)
[00:00] If you're a salesperson and you want you or your teams to start selling more, you want to pay attention to what I'm going to go over. So, I'm going to teach you something today called precision probing. Okay? So, we're going to give you some examples of precision probing and how you can use it for what you sell.
[00:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "What are the two biggest emotional drivers that cause a human being to want to change?"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Slide introdutório destacando os dois principais motivadores emocionais para mudança
[00:22] Now, the first thing I want to show you is right here. This is something that you have to understand. Okay? What are the two biggest emotional drivers that cause a human being to want to change? Number one is pain.
[00:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Pain - Emotional Driver #1"
NÚMEROS: 5%, 10%, 1-2% (percentuais de vendas fáceis)
VISUAL: Destaque visual para "Pain" como primeiro motivador emocional, possivelmente com ícones representando dor ou desconforto
[00:38] Now, your laydown sales that come to you, they might be in this situation where they already feel so much pain. So, they feel more pain with their problems than they do with the pain of letting go of that money it takes to solve those problems. Those are lay down sales. But how many of your how many prospects you talk to are laydowns? 5%. If that, maybe 10%, probably unlikely. It's probably 5% or less. Maybe 1 or 2%. Okay.
[01:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Fear - Emotional Driver #2"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: Destaque visual para "Fear" como segundo motivador emocional, possivelmente com elementos visuais representando medo ou preocupação futura
[01:10] Now the second way a prospect wants to change emotional driver that caused them to change is that they have a fear that this pain is going to keep happening or the pain could happen. Let's say they don't have much pain now. Let's say if you sell life insurance that's the the biggest industry bet we're training. They might not necessarily have a lot of pain around this now unless a relative passed away or friend that didn't have a policy and they felt the pain of that family right because they they were not financially protected.
[01:43] Now they had to get into debt. They had to move out of their house. They might feel pain that way, but a lot of that is a fear of future pain that it could happen down the road if they don't do something now.
[01:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Pain + Fear = Maximum Motivation"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Diagrama ou equação visual mostrando a combinação de dor presente e medo futuro como fórmula para motivação máxima de compra
[01:57] So, you want them to feel both. If you only have them feeling pain right now, but not really a fear that it's going to keep happening, they're not going to feel that much need. Okay? And if you only get them to fear that, you know, there could be a time where they need it, it's not likely. Well, you're still going to get some people, but you're going to lose a lot of people as well. So, we want them to feel both. Okay? And I'm going to show you to do that. Okay.
[02:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "What causes a prospect to relive their pain?"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Pergunta provocativa destacada, possivelmente com ponto de interrogação visual ou ícone de questionamento
[02:30] Now, what causes a prospect to relive their pain are not surface level questions like, uh, can you tell me two challenges that you're having? Okay, that is a very surface level vague question. Okay, that the prospect knows where that leads.
[02:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Surface Level Questions = Surface Level Answers"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Comparação visual mostrando perguntas vagas gerando respostas vagas, possivelmente com ícones de superfície rasa vs profundidade
[02:50] So, that's why you get a lot of surface level, vague, generalized answers from your prospects because you've been taught how to ask vague, generalized, service level questions. It's no, there's no doubt why they give you vague responses. Okay? So, when you ask questions, you have to be able to know how to pro how to clarify and probe off those questions to get them to feel pain. Okay.
[03:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Why Use Precision Probing?"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Título destacado introduzindo o conceito principal de "Precision Probing"
[03:17] Now, why do we use precision probing? And I'm going to show you some examples here in a second. Remember, we want to help them relive their pain of their current situation and even their past history. Okay? That drives change. Okay?
[03:32] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Self-Persuasion > Your Persuasion"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Comparação visual mostrando auto-persuasão sendo mais eficaz que persuasão externa, possivelmente com setas ou gráfico comparativo
[03:35] And we want them to tell themselves why they want to change, which I'm going to show you to do that in a second. That is self-persuasion. Okay? Is it more persuasive if you tell the prospect why they should change? Or is it more persuasive if your questions allow them to tell themselves why they feel they need to change? Which is going to be more persuasive? Of course, the second form where the prospect is telling themselves why they need to change.
[04:02] Now, they're not just going to tell themselves they need to change without you knowing what questions to ask that trigger that and knowing how to use your tonality, okay, to trigger that emotional response. Those are learn skills you have to acquire. Prospects aren't just going to tell you all that unless, like I said, they're a laydown sale. Okay?
[04:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Titanic Iceberg Analogy"
NÚMEROS: 90%, 300-400 meters
VISUAL: Imagem ou ilustração de iceberg mostrando pequena parte visível acima da água e grande massa submersa, representando problemas ocultos do prospect
[04:25] Uh this also causes them to go below the surface. So, like I always like, let's go back to the movie Titanic. Okay? when they hit that iceberg, you really you only see like a small portion of that iceberg, right? That's what they saw on the surface. But what was below it, you know, 300, 400 meters of iceberg, and that's where most prospects are.
[04:48] If you only ask surface level questions, you're only getting to see what's up on the surface. You're not seeing the 90% that's buried below the surface. They don't trust you enough yet to tell you or to open up to you about. Okay, that can change once you learn what I'm about to show you.
[05:06] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Precision Probing Examples"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Título de seção introduzindo exemplos práticos, possivelmente com ícone de lupa ou ferramenta de análise
[05:08] Let me give you a few examples. And this is all contextual. There are going to be tweaks depending on what you sell and depending on what they say and depending on what questions you ask. I'm just going to show you a few examples right now.
[05:23] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Example #1: Emotional Word Detection"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Exemplo destacando palavras emocionais como "stressed", com possivelmente destaque visual em vermelho ou negrito
[05:25] Let's say if you're a prospect throughout that discovery part of the conversation, whether you sell BTOC and you're in a one call close or if you sell business to business and you're in a multi-close uh multi-close option, you could be an enterprise where it takes a year. So, let's say in that conversation, the prospect says, "Ah, I just I feel stressed about." And they repeat the problem.
[05:50] I feel stress about whatever the problem is. I feel stress, let's say if you're selling for a marketing agency. I just feel stressed with the leads. like sales people just they're not they're just not getting through to them. Like people aren't even picking up the phone. They just got a lot of stress around this.
[06:09] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Repeat Back Emotional Words"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Lista de palavras emocionais: stressed, tension, pressure, frustrated, annoyed, anxiety - possivelmente com destaque ou círculos ao redor
[06:11] Now what you can do rather than just going to the next surface level question is I can simply repeat back. So, if a prospect ever gives you an emotional word like they like stressed or tension or pressure or frustrated or I'm annoyed by, you know, this situation or I I I'm, you know, I'm I have a lot of anxiety around, you know, XYZ problem.
[06:37] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Concerned/Confused Tone"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Demonstração visual de tonalidade, possivelmente com ondas sonoras ou ícones de voz mostrando tom preocupado/confuso
[06:40] All you have to do to get them to open up is simply repeat back the emotional word with a concern tone. Like a concerned/confused tone. I'm going to teach you a little bit about tonality right here. This needs to be said in a concerned slash confused tone. Now, when I say confused tone, I don't mean I'm 109 years old and I'm suffering from dementia. Like, I don't know what's going on. I'm confused by I don't know what they mean by stressed.
[07:08] Because do you know what they mean by stressed? It probably means something different to you and to another prospect than it does this prospect. It doesn't mean the same thing, right?
[07:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Stressed? How do you mean by stressed?"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Exemplo de script destacado com ênfase na pergunta reflexiva, possivelmente com aspas ou balão de diálogo
[07:20] So, I can say, "Oh, hold on. Stress? How do you mean by stressed? Stressed. Okay. Notice my tone sounds like I'm concerned for this and I'm confused. I'm not understanding what you mean by that. Okay. So, I'm con stressed or I can say tension. I'm feeling so much tension with XYZ uh challenge rabbit tension.
[07:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Emotional Dump = Good"
NÚMEROS: 7 minutes (tempo de exemplo)
VISUAL: Representação visual de prospect "despejando" emoções, possivelmente com ícone de pessoa falando ou desabafando
[07:47] And notice when you just repeat back that word in that kind of concerned slash I'm not understanding tone, that confused tone, your prospect will emotionally dump. They'll be like, "Oh gosh, you have no idea." and they'll just start emotionally dumping. Okay, that is good for you. Now, when they emotionally dump, you've got to know how to steer that conversation. You can't just let them emotionally dump for seven minutes and it goes nowhere.
[08:13] You have to steer it with more clarifying and probing questions, which I'll show you a few here in a second. Okay, let's say I'm like stressed. Yeah, just the other day it happened again when
[08:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Yeah, just the other day it happened again when..."
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Script de exemplo mostrando resposta típica do prospect, possivelmente em formato de citação ou balão de fala
[08:27] Okay, and now they start to tell you about something. Yeah, just the other day it happened again when Now once I find out that Okay. And now they could say different things here. I'm just saying they say, "Yeah, just the other day it happened again when" and they start explaining it. They could explain it differently. It just depends on what you sell and the context.
[08:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "NPQ Probing Question: How long has that been going on for?"
NÚMEROS: 6 months (exemplo usado), 6 years (exemplo usado)
VISUAL: Pergunta destacada como técnica NPQ (Neuro-Persuasive Questioning), possivelmente com destaque ou caixa ao redor
[08:50] Then I can come back and say, "This is a NPQ probing question right here." Okay, so write this down. Then I can say, "Oh, how long has that been going on for?" Now, why would I ask them how long this problem or problems has been going on for? because it helps them realize how long they've had the problem without changing it for.
[09:12] So, I'm getting them to internalize in their brain how long this issue or this stress they've had to deal with has been going on for. See how I'm getting them emotionally involved? Oh, it's been going on, let's say, for 6 months.
[09:27] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "So, that's been going on for the past six months. Has it had a... Has it had an impact on you?"
NÚMEROS: 6 months
VISUAL: Script mostrando pausas verbais (representadas por "..."), demonstrando técnica de desaceleração da fala
[09:30] So, that's been going on for the past six months. Has it had a has it had a impact on you? Now, notice how I slowed that down. So, you've been having I can repeat back the problem. So, you've been having to manually dictate your notes the last 6 years at that hospital. Like, has that had a Has that had an impact on you?
[09:53] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Verbal Pauses = Time to Internalize"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Demonstração visual de pausas na fala, possivelmente com pontos ou espaços representando silêncios estratégicos
[09:55] Now, I'm also Why are these periods here? Because this is a verbal pause. Because if I say this too fast, so this has been going on for six months. Has that had an impact on you? Well, yeah. I mean, it's not that bad.
[10:10] A lot of times if you ask your questions too the I would say the biggest reason why your prospects give you vague generalized surface level answers is because you ask the question so fast you give them no time to internalize and think deeper about what you're asking.
[10:28] So you're going to get a knee-jerk response which is not going to cause them to feel much pain. It's not going to cause them to get in that emotional state.
[10:36] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Alternative: What's that doing to you?"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Script alternativo destacado com ênfase na tonalidade suave e preocupada
[10:38] Now this thing right here. So if you notice by their tonality or their body language, if you can see them, that they're not they are emotionally opening up to you already. So rather than me saying this has been going on for XYZ months or years, has that had a has that had an impact on you? If they've already emotionally opened up before that, like they have just shared everything, they emotionally open up, I simply lean and say, "What's that doing to you?"
[11:07] Now notice how my tone shifts down into that soft concerned tone. Okay. I if they've already opened up emotionally, I don't necessarily need to say, "Has that had a has that had an impact on you?" I can simply go into, "What's that doing to you? What's this doing to you?" See, boom, right there. And emotions fly. Okay.
[11:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Example #2: Specific Example Request"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Novo exemplo destacado mostrando técnica de solicitar exemplos específicos
[11:30] Uh Okay. Or, "Yeah, we're having some problems with XYZ and ABC." Okay. So, this is a different example. So, we're having some issues with XYZ and ABC. Can you give me a specific example of when that's actually happened?
[11:46] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Taking Them to the Past"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Representação visual de linha do tempo ou seta apontando para trás, simbolizando revisitar experiências passadas
[11:48] See, now I'm taking them back to what? The past, their past history about when these problems have happened. Okay? So now I'm taking them back to that.
[11:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Question Sequence: Company → Personal"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Sequência visual mostrando progressão de perguntas: primeiro sobre empresa/departamento, depois pessoal
[12:00] Now I'm getting them to say to themselves how it affects their company or their department. Then I'm going to go back around. How does it affect you personally though? Then how do you affect you personally? I don't want to ask this question first. How does that affect you personally though to like a C-level executive or an A type? Because that's a much harder question for them to open up emotionally.
[12:25] This how does it affect your company or department that happens is easier for them to open up about that than their personal selves. That's why I'm putting that second. This is more emotional here. Okay? So, I'm framing that the right way. This is more logical.
[12:41] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Logical → Emotional Progression"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Diagrama mostrando progressão de perguntas lógicas para emocionais, possivelmente com setas ou gradiente
[12:43] Now, sometimes they can give you emotional answers when you ask this type of question, but most of the time it's going to be more logical and that leads for me to be able to open them up emotionally with that second question. You with me on that? Okay, good.
[13:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Questions? Text: 480-637-2944"
NÚMEROS: 480-637-2944
VISUAL: Número de telefone destacado com call-to-action para contato via texto
[13:02] Now, if you have questions about what I just went over, the easiest way to get a hold of me is simply to text me at this number right here. Okay, so hopefully that helped you today with precision probing.
[13:15] I want you to, you know, be able to understand how to get your prospects to feel that pain of their current situation and then have a fear of continuous pain and a fear of future pain if they don't change your situation by purchasing what you're offering that would actually solve those problems or challenges or emotional needs.
[13:37] Maybe you sell $50,000 Birkin bags as my girlfriend would say that she wants one. Okay, I I'm just joking. You know what I mean by that if you're a guy now.
[13:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Contact: 480-637-2944"
NÚMEROS: 480-637-2944, 3-4 sales trainers, 1-1.5 hours daily
VISUAL: Informações de contato com detalhes sobre processo de resposta em sala de conferência
[13:50] Uh, so easiest way to get a hold of me if you have questions about, hey, how do I apply this to my industry or what do I do if the prospect says this? How can I clarify and probe? Uh, easiest way is just to simply text me at this number 480-637-2944 and me and usually about three or four sales trainers, sometimes more. We'll go into a conference room about an hour, hour and a half a day. We actually answer your questions.
[14:18] So, hope that helped you. Enjoy that. Go use it. And if you have questions, simply text us and we'll get back to you. Take care.
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
5% ou menos dos prospects são "vendas fáceis" (laydowns)
1-2% taxa mais realista de laydown sales
90% dos problemas do prospect estão "abaixo da superfície"
300-400 metros de profundidade do iceberg (analogia Titanic)
6 meses / 6 anos - exemplos de duração de problemas
7 minutos - tempo máximo para deixar prospect desabafar sem direcionamento
$50,000 - exemplo de produto de luxo (Birkin bags)
480-637-2944 - número de contato direto
3-4 sales trainers - equipe de suporte
1-1.5 horas diárias - tempo dedicado para responder perguntas
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Precision Probing - Técnica central de questionamento profundo
Dois Motivadores Emocionais:
Pain (Dor presente)
Fear (Medo de dor futura/contínua)
Self-Persuasion - Fazer o prospect se convencer através de suas próprias respostas
Repetição de Palavras Emocionais - Técnica de eco com tonalidade preocupada/confusa
NPQ (Neuro-Persuasive Questioning) - Perguntas que ativam internalização
Pausas Verbais Estratégicas - Dar tempo para o prospect processar emocionalmente
Sequência Lógico → Emocional - Começar com impacto na empresa, depois pessoal
Analogia do Iceberg do Titanic - Acessar os 90% de problemas ocultos
ESTRUTURA:
Fase 1: Detecção de Palavras Emocionais
Identificar: stressed, tension, pressure, frustrated, annoyed, anxiety
Repetir a palavra com tom preocupado/confuso
Permitir "emotional dump" controlado
Fase 2: Questionamento Temporal
"How long has that been going on for?"
Fazer prospect internalizar duração do problema
Criar consciência de inação prolongada
Fase 3: Questionamento de Impacto
"Has it had a... has it had an impact on you?"
Alternativa: "What's that doing to you?" (tom suave e preocupado)
Usar pausas verbais para permitir processamento emocional
Fase 4: Solicitação de Exemplos Específicos
"Can you give me a specific example of when that's actually happened?"
Levar prospect de volta ao passado
Reviver experiências dolorosas
Fase 5: Progressão Empresa → Pessoal
Primeiro: "How does it affect your company/department?"
Depois: "How does it affect you personally though?"
Estratégia especialmente eficaz com C-levels e tipos A
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Não aplicável - vídeo focado em técnicas de vendas, não em métricas financeiras específicas
DURAÇÃO: 14:18 FORMATO: Vídeo educacional de treinamento em vendas com apresentação em tela
